title: No Disintegrations (West End Games)
content:
{{Top|rwm|ref|reb|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the [[West End Games]] sourcebook|the [[Fantasy Flight Games]] [[No Disintegrations (Fantasy Flight Games)|sourcebook]]|linebreak=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:NoDisint.jpg]] |title=''No Disintegrations'' |author=[[Kevin M. MacGregor]], [[Sandra Childress]], [[Christopher John Parks]], [[Chuck Truett]], [[Chris Hind]] |cover artist= |illustrator=[[Jason Palmer]], [[Marshall Andrews III]], [[Joe Corroney]], [[Storn Cook]], [[Christina Wald]] |editor=[[Bill Smith]] |publisher=[[West End Games]] |release date=[[March]] [[1997]]{{InsiderCite|33|The Jawa Trader|Jawa Trader}} |type=Softcover |pages=95 |dimensions= |weight= |isbn=0874312965 |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} {{Quote|There will be a substantial reward for the one who finds the ''Millennium Falcon''. You are free to use any methods necessary, but I want them alive. No disintegrations.''"
"''As you wish.|Darth Vader and Boba Fett|{{Film|V}}}} '''''No Disintegrations''''' is a [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] sourcebook for bounty hunter adventures. Edited by [[Bill Smith]] and released in [[1997]], it gets its title from the line spoken by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. ==Publisher's summary== '''''ATTENTION BOUNTY HUNTERS!''''' ''Friendships betrayed, loves lost, belongings stolen...'' ''Those who have been wronged want justice. And they are willing to pay handsomely to get it.'' ''That's where you come in. You're a bounty hunter, ready for battle and afraid of no one. You earn your living by tracking down those who desperately do not want to be found. Cunning and dangerous, you are not to be trifled with. But are you good enough to stop these wanted criminals?'' '''''[[Narloch of Giju]]''. A Herglic gambler who has defrauded noble Lord Haakon of his lands, wealth, and honor.'' '''''[[Ort Hoogra-D'En]]''. A Ho'Din terrorist wanted for several bombings using fatal biological agents.'' '''''[[Hahz Fallone]]''. A wealthy business leader and suspected criminal operative responsible for the destruction of a primitive alien civilization.'' ''Imperial officials and private sponsors are paying top credits for these and other bounties.'' ==Contents== *[[Gone To Ground]] by [[Kevin M.
